title: verify gate must run from defaults when playbook config is absent

﻿## Task
The verify gate must verify by default. Today `gateVerify` returns early when `playbook.config.json` is absent, even though `loadConfig` has a default `verifyCommand`. That creates a silent path where a project without config can move active -> review without the CLI running verify.

Fix the gate:
1. `active -> review` always loads config defaults and runs the configured `verifyCommand` unless a typed/configured gate setting explicitly disables it.
2. Remove the ad hoc regex escape hatch for `enforceVerifyOnReview`; if disabling verify is allowed at all, it must be schema/config-backed and visible in `doctor`/`check self`.
3. The refusal must name the verify command that failed.
4. Cover missing-config repos and malformed-config repos separately: defaults run when absent; malformed config refuses clearly.

## RED test
Add a transition/red-team test named exactly: "move to review runs the default verify command when config is absent". In a fixture repo with no `playbook.config.json`, set up a failing default verify command path or fixture npm verify, attempt active -> review, and assert the move is refused because verify ran.
Expected failure cause (literal string in the output): the test name "move to review runs the default verify command when config is absent".

## Reuse
`gateVerify`, `loadConfig`, `GATE-VERIFY-001`, `STORE-001` schema validation when it lands, red-team suite.

## Stop conditions
Leaving a silent no-config bypass; keeping regex config parsing; weakening verify to pass tests; touching files outside the write_set.

## Evidence required at close
red-test-output, verify-root, final-sha.
